question_text: A car of mass 1000 kg is being pulled by a rope with a tension force of 4000 N at an angle of 30° above the horizontal. Air resistance acts horizontally with a force of 500 N opposing the motion. Which of the following best describes the direction of the resultant force on the car?

options.label: B
options.text: At an angle above the horizontal (forward and upward)

options.label: D
options.text: At an angle below the horizontal (forward and downward)

explanation: The tension force has both horizontal and vertical components. The horizontal component (4000 N × cos 30° ≈ 3464 N) minus air resistance (500 N) gives a net horizontal force of about 3000 N forward. The vertical component (4000 N × sin 30° = 2000 N) acts upward unopposed. The resultant combines both, pointing forward and upward at an angle.
